news.title:
AB Capital, L&T Finance added to MSCI Index; IRCTC exits.
AB Capital, L&T Finance, AU Bank set for inflows; IRCTC faces outflows.
news.summary:

news.title:
IRCTC to be excluded from MSCI index in February rejig.
Expected impact on IRCTC stock is USD 148M (Rs 13.5B).
news.summary:
IRCTC to be excluded from MSCI index in February rejig.
Expected impact on IRCTC stock is USD 148M (Rs 13.5B).

news.title:
IRCTC Q3 results show declining margins; focus on long-term topline growth guidance.
Sanjay Kumar Jain discusses reasons behind margin declines and future growth outlook.
news.summary:
IRCTC Q3 results show declining margins; focus on long-term topline growth guidance.
Sanjay Kumar Jain discusses reasons behind margin declines and future growth outlook.

news.title:
Coal India & Biocon reported mixed results; Coal India's profit beat estimates while Biocon's net profit rose YoY despite missing estimates.
IRCTC, Indian Hotels, and Engineers India posted strong revenue/profit growth, while Schneider Electric & Petronet LNG showed steady performance.
news.summary:
Coal India & Biocon reported mixed results; Coal India's profit beat estimates while Biocon's net profit rose YoY despite missing estimates.
IRCTC, Indian Hotels, and Engineers India posted strong revenue/profit growth, while Schneider Electric & Petronet LNG showed steady performance.

news.title: IRCTC declares a dividend of ₹3.5 per share.
news.summary: IRCTC declares a dividend of ₹3.5 per share.

news.title:
IRCTC Q3 EBITDA rose to ₹4.65B from ₹4.17B YoY, showing growth.
EBITDA margin fell to 32.1% from 34.0% YoY, indicating reduced profitability.
news.summary:
IRCTC Q3 EBITDA rose to ₹4.65B from ₹4.17B YoY, showing growth.
EBITDA margin fell to 32.1% from 34.0% YoY, indicating reduced profitability.

news.title:
IRCTC's Board approved unaudited financial results for Q3 and 9 months ending Dec 31, 2025, along with a Rs 3.50/share interim dividend.
Feb 20, 2026, is set as the record date for dividend eligibility; financials adhere to Indian Accounting Standards.
news.summary:
IRCTC's Board approved unaudited financial results for Q3 and 9 months ending Dec 31, 2025, along with a Rs 3.50/share interim dividend.
Feb 20, 2026, is set as the record date for dividend eligibility; financials adhere to Indian Accounting Standards.

news.title:
AU Small Finance Bank to see $172M inflows post MSCI weight increase affecting 15.6M shares.
IRCTC excluded from MSCI index with $142M outflows; India's index weight stays at 14.1%.
news.summary:
AU Small Finance Bank to see $172M inflows post MSCI weight increase affecting 15.6M shares.
IRCTC excluded from MSCI index with $142M outflows; India's index weight stays at 14.1%.

news.title:
IRCTC removed from MSCI index; AB Capital, L&T Finance added.
Review of potential market impacts on these stocks is suggested.
news.summary:
IRCTC removed from MSCI index; AB Capital, L&T Finance added.
Review of potential market impacts on these stocks is suggested.

news.title:
Major Q3 results this week include Hindalco, ONGC, Britannia, M&M, and Ashok Leyland.
Other key companies reporting include Titan, IRCTC, Eicher Motors, Pfizer, and Apollo Hospitals.
news.summary:
Major Q3 results this week include Hindalco, ONGC, Britannia, M&M, and Ashok Leyland.

news.title:
IRCTC to host Q3 FY26 Earnings Conference Call on Feb 13, 2026, at 4 PM IST.
Call will feature management speakers; transcript/audio/video to be shared on IRCTC's website.
news.summary:
IRCTC to host Q3 FY26 Earnings Conference Call on Feb 13, 2026, at 4 PM IST.
Call will feature management speakers; transcript/audio/video to be shared on IRCTC's website.

news.title:
IRCTC received a GST DRC-07 Order for FY 2018-19 demanding ₹3.05 lakh, including ₹1.52 lakh penalty.
The penalty is litigative and can be contested before appellate authorities within legal timelines.
news.summary:
IRCTC received a GST DRC-07 Order for FY 2018-19 demanding ₹3.05 lakh, including ₹1.52 lakh penalty.
The penalty is litigative and can be contested before appellate authorities within legal timelines.

news.title: IRCTC and Ambuja are in focus as Sensex trades flat and Nifty near 26,200.
news.summary: IRCTC and Ambuja are in focus as Sensex trades flat and Nifty near 26,200.

news.title:
IRCTC gains over 1% as Indian Railways raises fares by up to ₹10.
NSE advance-decline ratio currently stands at 5:2.
news.summary:
IRCTC gains over 1% as Indian Railways raises fares by up to ₹10.
NSE advance-decline ratio currently stands at 5:2.

news.title:
IRCTC's Q2 FY26 revenue rose 7.7% YoY to Rs. 1,146 crore, driven by catering (Rs. 519.7 crore) and internet ticketing (Rs. 385.9 crore).
Tourism revenue grew 20.2% to Rs. 149.5 crore, while Rail Neer contributed Rs. 94.1 crore. IRCTC shows strong financials with 49% ROCE and 46.4% dividend payout.
news.summary:
IRCTC's Q2 FY26 revenue rose 7.7% YoY to Rs. 1,146 crore, driven by catering (Rs. 519.7 crore) and internet ticketing (Rs. 385.9 crore).
Tourism revenue grew 20.2% to Rs. 149.5 crore, while Rail Neer contributed Rs. 94.1 crore. IRCTC shows strong financials with 49% ROCE and 46.4% dividend payout.

news.title:
Tata Power shifted from coal-based power to green energy, renewables, and EV charging, focusing on sustainability.
IRCTC evolved from catering to a digital monopoly for ticket booking; MSTC moved from scrap trading to e-auction tech, and Bajaj Finance became a fintech leader.
news.summary:
Tata Power shifted from coal-based power to green energy, renewables, and EV charging, focusing on sustainability.
IRCTC evolved from catering to a digital monopoly for ticket booking; MSTC moved from scrap trading to e-auction tech, and Bajaj Finance became a fintech leader.

news.title:
IRCTC, Oil India, Info Edge, MRF announce dividends; Nov. 20 is the last day to qualify.
IRCTC Rs 5/share, Oil India Rs 3.5, Info Edge Rs 2.4, MRF Rs 3; dividends taxable for shareholders.
news.summary:
IRCTC, Oil India, Info Edge, MRF announce dividends; Nov. 20 is the last day to qualify.
IRCTC Rs 5/share, Oil India Rs 3.5, Info Edge Rs 2.4, MRF Rs 3; dividends taxable for shareholders.

news.title:
Indian Railway Catering & Tourism Corporation (NSE:IRCTC) will go ex-dividend on November 21; shareholders on record will receive a ₹5.00 dividend on December 12.
IRCTC has a trailing yield of 1.1% and has grown earnings 22% annually over five years, with dividends increasing 26% annually in six years.
news.summary:
Indian Railway Catering & Tourism Corporation (NSE:IRCTC) will go ex-dividend on November 21; shareholders on record will receive a ₹5.00 dividend on December 12.
IRCTC has a trailing yield of 1.1% and has grown earnings 22% annually over five years, with dividends increasing 26% annually in six years.

news.title:
Cochin Shipyard, Asian Paints, Ashok Leyland, and IRCTC among stocks trading ex-dividend next week starting 17 Nov 2025.
Key corporate actions include bonus issues, right issues, and income distributions by companies like Autoriders, Adani Enterprises, and IRB InvIT Fund.
news.summary:
Cochin Shipyard, Asian Paints, Ashok Leyland, and IRCTC among stocks trading ex-dividend next week starting 17 Nov 2025.
Key corporate actions include bonus issues, right issues, and income distributions by companies like Autoriders, Adani Enterprises, and IRB InvIT Fund.

IRCTC CEO highlights plans to double revenue in 3-5 years.
Big expansion plans outlined ahead following Q2 results.
news.summary:
